name: pctheory

metadata.name: pctheory
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)
metadata.summary: A package with functionality for atonal music theory

documentation:
# `pctheory`
`pctheory` is a Python library for using atonal music theory. It is useful for both computer-assisted composition in Python and analysis. Some of the many useful features include:
- Set-classes
    - Generate all abstract subsets of a set-class
    - Get Forte and Carter names for a set-class
    - Work with microtonal sets
    - Generate set-class-complexes
- Rows
    - Generate a twelve-tone matrix
    - Generate a rotational array
    - Generate an invariance matrix
    - Load a random all-interval, all-trichord, ten-trichord, or Babbit ten-trichord row
    
`pctheory` is much more specialized than `music21`, and offers substantially more functionality in atonal theory. Additionally, it does not use `music21` objects, so there is less computational overhead when using `pctheory`. It is not difficult to create `music21` objects from `pctheory` objects, which allows `pctheory` to be used along with `music21`.

## Installation
`pctheory` is a Python package and can be installed with the command `pip install pctheory` from the terminal on your computer (Command Prompt or PowerShell on Windows, Terminal on Linux or macOS). Note that you need Python 3.10 or newer.

## Quick start
To create ordered or unordered collections (like pitch-class sets and twelve-tone rows), you use basic Python data structures like `set` and `list`. You can then add `pctheory.pitch.PitchClass` objects. To work with set-classes, you create a `pctheory.pcset.SetClass` object.

## System Requirements
`pctheory` requires Python 3.10 or newer.

## Documentation
Documentation can be found at https://fleximeter.github.io/pc-pctheory.html.

## Copyright and license
`pctheory` is copyright © 2024 by Jeffrey Martin. This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version. This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details. To view the GNU General Public License v.3.0, visit https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.en.html.

functions.name: pctheory.contour.com
documentation:
The COM function for two contour pitches. Returns 1 if a < b, 0 if a == b, -1 if a > b.
\:param a: A contour pitch
\:param b: A contour pitch
\:return: The COM result

functions.name: pctheory.contour.com_mx
documentation:
Generates a COM matrix for two contour pitch segments.
\:param cseg1: A cseg
\:param cseg2: A cseg
\:return: The COM matrix

functions.name: pctheory.contour.invert
documentation:
Inverts a contour pitch segment.
\:param cseg: The cseg
\:return: The inverted cseg

functions.name: pctheory.contour.retrograde
documentation:
Retrogrades a contour pitch segment.
\:param cseg: The cseg
\:return: The retrograded cseg

functions.name: pctheory.contour.rotate
documentation:
Rotates a contour pitch segment.
\:param cseg: The cseg
\:param n: The index of rotation
\:return: The rotated cseg

functions.name: pctheory.contour.simplify
documentation:
Simplifies a contour pitch segment.
\:param cseg: A cseg
\:return: A simplified form of the cseg

documentation:

Represents a group of operators. Only compatible with mod 12 and mod 24 systems.

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.find_otos
documentation:
Gets all OTO transformations of pcseg1 that contain pcseg2 as an ordered subseg
\:param pcseg1: A pcseg
\:param pcseg2: A pcseg
\:return: A set of OTOs that transform pcseg1 so that it contains pcseg2.
Compatible with PitchClasses mod 12 and 24

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.find_utos
documentation:
Finds the UTOS that transform pcset1 so it contains pcset2. pcset2 can be a subset of pcset1.
\:param pcset1: A pcset
\:param pcset2: A pcset
\:return: A list of UTOS

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.get_otos12
documentation:
Gets chromatic OTOs (ROs)
\:return: A list of OTOs

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.get_otos24
documentation:
Gets microtonal OTOs
\:return: A list of microtonal OTOs

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.get_utos12
documentation:
Gets the twelve-tone UTOs (TTOs)
\:return: A dictionary of UTOs

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.get_utos24
documentation:
Gets the 24-tone UTOs (24TOs)
\:return: A dictionary of UTOs

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.left_multiply_utos
documentation:
Left-multiplies a list of UTOs
\:param args: A collection of UTOs (can be one argument as a list, or multiple UTOs separated by commas.
The highest index is evaluated first, and the lowest index is evaluated last.
\:param mod: The number of pcs in the system
\:return: The result

functions.name: pctheory.transformations.make_uto_list
documentation:
Makes a UTO list
\:param args: One or more tuples or lists representing UTOs
\:return: A UTO list

classes.name: pctheory.transformations.OTO

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.OTO.__init__
documentation:
Creates an OTO
\:param T: The index of transposition
\:param R: Whether or not to retrograde
\:param M: The multiplier

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.OTO.oto
documentation:
Gets the OTO as a tuple. Index 0 is the index of transposition, index 1 is whether or not to retrograde, and
index 2 is the multiplier.
\:return: The OTO

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.OTO.oto
documentation:
Sets the OTO using a tuple
\:param value: A tuple
\:return:

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.OTO.transform
documentation:
Transforms an item (can be a pitch-class, list, set, or any number of nestings of these objects)
\:param item: An item
\:return: The transformed item

Represents an ordered tone operator (OTO). If used with a twelve-tone row, it is a row operator (RO).
Objects of this class are subscriptable. [0] is the index of transposition. [1] is whether or not to
retrograde (0-no or 1-yes). [2] is the multiplier. Multiplication is performed first, then retrograding,
then transposition. These operators can be used with pcsegs.

classes.name: pctheory.transformations.UTO

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.UTO.__init__
documentation:
Creates a UTO
\:param T: The index of transposition
\:param M: The index of multiplication

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.UTO.uto
documentation:
Gets the UTO as a list. Index 0 is the index of transposition, and index 1
is the multiplier.
\:return: The UTO

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.UTO.uto
documentation:
Sets the UTO using a tuple
\:param value: A tuple
\:return:

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.UTO.cycles
documentation:
Gets the cycles of the UTO
\:param mod: The number of possible pcs in the system
\:return: The cycles, as a list of lists

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.UTO.inverse
documentation:
Gets the inverse of the UTO
\:param mod: The number of possible pcs in the system
\:return: The inverse

methods.name: pctheory.transformations.UTO.transform
documentation:
Transforms a pcset, pcseg, or pc
\:param item: A pcset, pcseg, or pc
\:return: The transformed item

Represents an unordered tone operator (UTO), which can be used as a twelve-tone operator (TTO)
or 24-tone operator (24TO). Objects of this class are subscriptable.
[0] is the index of transposition. [1] is the multiplier. Multiplication is performed first,
then transposition.

functions.name: pctheory.util.factor
documentation:
Factors a positive integer
\:param n: An integer
\:returns: A list of factors, in sorted order, including duplicates

functions.name: pctheory.util.lcm
documentation:
Computes the LCM of a list of positive integers
\:param integers: A list of positive integers
\:return: The LCM

functions.name: pctheory.util.map_to_chromatic
documentation:
Maps one sequence of items to another sequence of items. This is useful for doing
things like mapping scale degrees 0-6 to actual chromatic pitches. You only need
to provide a map for one octave, and all transpositions will be accounted for.
\:param scale_map: The scale map
\:param sequence: The sequence to map
\:return: The mapped sequence

functions.name: pctheory.util.norgard
documentation:
Generates the first n numbers of OEIS A004718 (Per Nørgård's infinity series)
\:param n: The number of terms to compute
\:return: The series

functions.name: pctheory.set_complex.assert_k
documentation:
Asserts that s and t are in a K-relationship
Source: Morris, "Class Notes for Atonal Music Theory," p. 49
\:param s: A set-class
\:param t: A set-class
\:return: A boolean

functions.name: pctheory.set_complex.assert_kh
documentation:
Asserts that s and t are in a Kh-relationship
Source: Morris, "Class Notes for Atonal Music Theory," p. 49
\:param s: A set-class
\:param t: A set-class
\:return: A boolean

functions.name: pctheory.set_complex.get_k12
documentation:
Gets a K-complex about a provided nexus set
\:param nexus: A nexus set
\:return: The K-complex

functions.name: pctheory.set_complex.get_kh12
documentation:
Gets a Kh-complex about a provided nexus set
\:param nexus: A nexus set
\:return: The Kh-complex

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.get_all_combinatorial_hexachord
documentation:
Gets an all-combinatorial hexachord (ACH) by name (A-F).
\:param name: The name of the hexachord (A-F)
\:return: The hexachord set-class
Only produces mod 12 SetClasses

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.get_complement
documentation:
Gets the complement of a pcset.
\:param pcset: A pcset
\:return: The complement pcset
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.get_complement_map_utos
documentation:
Gets all UTOs that map a pcset into its complement.
\:param pcset: A pcset
\:return: A set of UTOs
Compatible with PitchClasses mod 12 and 24

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.get_corpus
documentation:
Gets all transformations of a provided pcset.
\:param pcset: A pcset

Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

documentation:
Gets all UTOs that map a pcset into itself.
\:param pcset: A pcset

Compatible with PitchClasses mod 12 and 24

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.convert_to_pcset12
documentation:
Converts a microtonal pcset (mod 24) to a chromatic pcset (mod 12). Microtonal pitch classes
are rounded down to the nearest chromatic pitch class.
\:param args: A microtonal pcset (mod 24)
\:return: A chromatic pcset (mod 12)

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.convert_to_pcset24
documentation:
Converts a chromatic pcset (mod 12) to a microtonal pcset (mod 24).
\:param args: A chromatic pcset (mod 12)
\:return: A microtonal pcset (mod 24)

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.invert
documentation:
Inverts a pcset.
\:param pcset: The pcset
\:return: The inverted pcset
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.is_all_combinatorial_hexachord
documentation:
Whether or not a pcset is an all-combinatorial hexachord.
\:param pcset: A pcset
\:return: True or False
Only compatible with mod 12 SetClasses

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.make_pcset12
documentation:
Makes a chromatic pcset (mod 12).
\:param args: Integers that represent pitch classes
\:return: A pcset

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.make_pcset24
documentation:
Makes a microtonal pcset (mod 24).
\:param args: Integers that represent pitch classes
\:return: A pcset

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.make_subset_graph
documentation:
Makes a subset graph.
\:param set_class: A set-class
\:param smallest_cardinality: The smallest cardinality to include in the graph
\:param show_graph: Whether or not to generate a visualization of the graph
\:param size: The size of the visualized graph
\:return: A graph

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.multiply
documentation:
Multiplies a pcset.
\:param pcset: The pcset
\:param n: The multiplier
\:return: The multiplied pcset
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.partitions2
documentation:
Gets all partitions of a pcset (size 2 or 1).
\:param pcset: A pcset
\:return: A list of all partitions
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.permutations
documentation:
Generates all permutations of a pcset. Uses a swapping notion derived from the Bauer-Mengelberg/Ferentz algorithm
for generating all-interval twelve-tone rows.
Note: The number of permutations will be n! where n is the length of the pcset. The amount of pcsegs is therefore
O(n!). You may not want to try generating all permutations of a twelve-note set.
You have been warned.
\:param pcs: A pcset
\:return: A list of pcsegs
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.set_class_filter12
documentation:
Filters a list of pcsets.
\:param name: The name to find
\:param sets: A list of sets to filter
\:return: A filtered list
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos. For pcsets of modulo 12, also supports Forte and Morris names.

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.subsets
documentation:
Gets all subsets of a pcset, using the bit masking solution from
<https://afteracademy.com/blog/print-all-subsets-of-a-given-set>
\:param pcset: A pcset
\:return: A list containing all subsets of the pcset

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.transform
documentation:
Transforms a pcset with a provided transformation string.
\- Tn: transpose
\- I: invert
\- Mn: multiply
\:param pcset: The pcset to transform
\:param string: The transformation string
\:return: The transformed pcset

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.transpose
documentation:
Transposes a pcset.
\:param pcset: The pcset
\:param n: The index of transposition
\:return: The transposed pcset
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.transpositional_combination
documentation:
Transpositionally combines (TC) two pcsets. This is Boulez's "multiplication."
\:param pcset1: A pcset
\:param pcset2: A pcset
\:return: The TC pcset
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcset.visualize
documentation:
Visualizes a pcset.
\:param pcset: A pcset
\:return: A visualization
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

classes.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.__init__
documentation:
Creates a SetClass.
\:param pcset: A pcset to initialize the SetClass

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.derived_core
documentation:
Gets derived core associations.
\:return: The derived core associations (or None if not derived core)

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.dsym
documentation:
Gets the degree of symmetry of the set-class.
\:return: The degree of symmetry

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.ic_vector
documentation:
Gets the IC vector.
\:return: The IC vector

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.ic_vector_long
documentation:
Gets the IC vector in long format.
\:return: The IC vector in long format

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.ic_vector_str
documentation:
Gets the IC vector as a string.
\:return: The IC vector

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.ic_vector_long_str
documentation:
Gets the IC vector in long format as a string.
\:return: The IC vector in long format

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.is_z_relation
documentation:
Whether or not this set-class is Z-related to another set-class.
\:return: A boolean

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.mod
documentation:
Gets the modulo for PCs in this SetClass.
\:return: The modulo

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.name_carter
documentation:
Gets the Carter name for a set-class.
\:return: The Carter name

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.name_forte
documentation:
Gets the Forte name for a set-class.
\:return: The Forte name

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.name_morris
documentation:
Gets the Morris name for a set-class.
\:return: The Morris name

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.name_prime
documentation:
Gets the prime-form name (Rahn) for a set-class.
\:return: The prime-form name

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.num_forte
documentation:
Get the number part of the Forte name.
\:return: The number part of the Forte name

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.pcset
documentation:
Gets the pcset prime form.
\:return: The pcset prime form

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.pcset
documentation:
Updates the pcset prime form based on an existing pcset or pcseg.
\:param value: The new pcset or pcseg
\:return:

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.weight_right
documentation:
Whether or not to weight from the right.
\:return: A Boolean

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.weight_right
documentation:
Whether or not to weight from the right.
\:param value: A Boolean
\:return:

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.calculate_prime_form
documentation:
Calculates the prime form of a pcset.
\:param pcset: The pcset
\:param weight_from_right: Whether or not to pack from the right
\:param pc_mod: The PitchClass mod value to use
\:return: The prime form

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.contains_abstract_subset
documentation:
Determines if a set-class is an abstract subset of this set-class.
\:param sc: A set-class
\:return: A boolean

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.get_abstract_complement
documentation:
Gets the abstract complement of the SetClass.
\:return: The abstract complement SetClass

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.get_invariance_vector
documentation:
Gets the invariance vector of the SetClass
\:return: The invariance vector, or None if the SetClass has a PitchClass modulo other than 12

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.get_abstract_subset_classes
documentation:
Gets a set of subset-classes contained in this SetClass.
:return:

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.get_partition2_subset_classes
documentation:
Gets a set of set-class partitions of this SetClass.
:return:

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.get_set_classes12
documentation:
Gets the chromatic set-classes.
\:param cardinalities: A list of cardinalities if you don't want the entire list of 224 set-classes
\:return: A list of the chromatic set-classes

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.get_z_relation
documentation:
Gets the Z-relation of the SetClass.
\:return: The Z-relation of the SetClass

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.is_all_combinatorial_hexachord
documentation:
Whether or not the SetClass is an all-combinatorial hexachord.
\:return: True or False

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.is_valid_name
documentation:
Determines if a chromatic (mod 12) set-class name is valid. Validates prime form, Forte, and Morris names.
Prime form name format: [xxxx]
Forte name format: x-x
Morris name format: (x-x)[xxxx]
\:param name: The name
\:return: A boolean

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass.load_from_name
documentation:
Loads a set-class from a prime-form, Morris, or Forte name.
\:param name: The name

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass._make_names
documentation:
Makes the names for the set-class.
:return:

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass._weight_from_right
documentation:
Weights pclists from the right.
\:param pclists: Pclists
\:param pc_mod: The PitchClass mod value to use
\:return: The most weighted form

methods.name: pctheory.pcset.SetClass._weight_left
documentation:
Weights pclists left.
\:param pclists: Pclists
\:param pc_mod: The PitchClass mod value to use
\:return: The most weighted form
Represents a pc-set-class.

functions.name: pctheory.pset.calculate_pm_similarity
documentation:
Gets the pitch-measure (PM) similarity between pset1 and pset2
\:param pset1: A pset
\:param pset2: A pset
\:param ic_roster1: The ic_roster for pset 1. If None, will be calculated.
\:param ic_roster2: The ic_roster for pset 2. If None, will be calculated.
\:return: The PM similarity as a tuple of integers
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.generate_random_pset_realizations
documentation:
Generates random pset realizations of a given pcset,
within the specified upper and lower boundaries
\:param pcset: The pcset to realize
\:param lower_boundary: The lower boundary
\:param upper_boundary: The upper boundary
\:param num_realizations: The number of random realizations to generate
\:param num_duplicate_pitches: The number of additional duplicate pitches to include (for doubling)
\:param filter_func: A function for filtering the pset realizations to force them to match specified criteria
\:return: One or more random pset realizations of the pcset within the given boundaries. If the number of realizations
is greater than 1, returns a list of psets. Otherwise returns a single pset.
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.get_fb_class
documentation:
Gets the FB-class of a pset
\:param pset: The pset
\:param p0: The lowest pitch
\:return: The FB-class as a list of integers
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.get_ic_matrix
documentation:
Gets the pitch ic-matrix
\:param pset: The pset
\:return: The ic-matrix as a list of lists
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.get_ic_roster
documentation:
Gets the pitch ic-roster
\:param pset: The pset
\:return: The ic-roster as a dictionary
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.get_pcint_class
documentation:
Gets the PCINT-class of a pset
\:param pset: The pset
\:return: The PCINT-class as a list of integers
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.get_set_class
documentation:
Gets the set-class of a pset
\:param pset: The pset
\:return: The set-class as a list of integers
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.invert
documentation:
Inverts a pset
\:param pset: The pset
\:return: The inverted pset
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.make_pset12
documentation:
Makes a pset
\:param args: Pitches
\:return: A pset
Compatible only with chromatic psegs

functions.name: pctheory.pset.make_pset24
documentation:
Makes a pset
\:param args: Pitches
\:return: A pset
Compatible only with microtonal psegs

functions.name: pctheory.pset.subsets
documentation:
Gets all subsets of a pset, using the bit masking solution from
<https://afteracademy.com/blog/print-all-subsets-of-a-given-set>
\:param pset: A pset
\:return: A list containing all subsets of the pset
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.to_pcset
documentation:
Makes a pcset out of a pset
\:param pset: A pset
\:return: A pcset
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.transform
documentation:
Transforms a pset
\:param pset: A pset
\:param transformation: A transformation
\:return: The transformed set
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pset.transpose
documentation:
Transposes a pset
\:param pset: The pset
\:param n: The index of transposition
\:return: The transposed pset
Compatible with all Pitch modulos

classes.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Sieve
\:param tuples: A collection of tuples to add to the sieve.
\:param base_pitch: Pitch 0 for the sieve. This pitch does not actually have to be
in the sieve - it will just serve as the 0 reference point.
\:param pc_mod: The pitch-class mod of the Sieve

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.base_pitch
documentation:
The base pitch of the Sieve (pitch 0)
\:return: The base pitch

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.intervals
documentation:
The intervallic succession of the Sieve
\:return: The intervallic succession

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.pc_mod
documentation:
The pitch class mod of the Sieve (which specifies the pitch type)
\:return: The pitch class mod

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.period
documentation:
The period of the Sieve
\:return: The period

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.tuples
documentation:
The tuples in the Sieve
\:return: The tuples

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.add_tuples
documentation:
Adds one or more tuples to the Sieve12
\:param args: One or more tuples
\:return: None

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.get_range
documentation:
Gets all pitches in the sieve between p0 and p1
\:param p0: The low pitch
\:param p1: The high pitch
\:return: A pset

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.intersection
documentation:
Intersects two Sieves
\:param sieve: A Sieve
\:return: A new Sieve. It will have the same base pitch as self.

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.is_in_sieve
documentation:
Whether or not a pitch or pset is in the sieve
\:param p: A pitch (Pitch or int) or pset
\:return: True or False

methods.name: pctheory.pset.Sieve.union
documentation:
Unions two Sieves
\:param sieve: A Sieve
\:return: A new Sieve. It will have the same base pitch as self.
Represents a Xenakis sieve. Compatible with all Pitch modulos.

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.adjacent_search
documentation:
Performs an adjacent search on a corpus of pcsegs. Returns all pcsegs in the corpus that contain pcseg as an ordered sublist.
\:param pcseg: A pcseg to search for
\:param corpus: The corpus of pcsegs to search
\:return: A sublist of pcsegs from the corpus that have pcseg in them. If an entry in the original corpus contains pcseg more than once,
it will only be included once in this sublist.
If no entries have pcseg in them, returns an empty list.

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.are_combinatorial2
documentation:
Determines if two rows are hexachordally combinatorial.
\:param row1: A row
\:param row2: A row
\:return: True or False
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.are_combinatorial3
documentation:
Determines if three rows are tetrachordally combinatorial.
\:param row1: A row
\:param row2: A row
\:param row3: A row
\:return: True or False
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.are_combinatorial4
documentation:
Determines if four rows are trichordally combinatorial.
\:param row1: A row
\:param row2: A row
\:param row3: A row
\:param row4: A row
\:return: True or False
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.bip_n
documentation:
Gets the BIP_n of a list of set-classes generated by IMB_n.
\:param imb: The IMB_n list
\:return: The BIP_n
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.create_ormap
documentation:
Creates the ORMAP of a row.
\:param pcseg: A row
\:return: The ORMAP
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_pcseg12_from_interval_list
documentation:
Generates a pcseg from an interval list.
\:param interval_list: The interval list
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: A pcseg
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_pcseg24_from_interval_list
documentation:
Generates a pcseg from an interval list.
\:param interval_list: The interval list
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: A pcseg
Compatible only with microtonal pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_random_all_interval_row
documentation:
Generates a random all-interval row.
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: An all-interval row
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_random_all_trichord_row
documentation:
Generates a random all-trichord row.
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: An all-trichord row
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_random_all_trichord_babbitt_row
documentation:
Generates a random all-trichord (Babbitt) row.
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: An all-trichord (Babbitt) row
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_random_pcseg12
documentation:
Generates a random pcseg.
\:param length: The length of the pcseg
\:param non_duplicative: Whether or not duplicate pcs may occur (must be True to generate a row)
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: A random pcseg
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_random_pcseg24
documentation:
Generates a random pcseg.
\:param length: The length of the pcseg
\:param non_duplicative: Whether or not duplicate pcs may occur (must be True to generate a row)
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: A random pcseg
Compatible only with microtonal pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_random_pcseg_from_pcset
documentation:
Generates a random pcseg from a pcset.
\:param pcset: A pcset
\:return: A pcseg
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.generate_random_ten_trichord_row
documentation:
Generates a random ten-trichord row.
\:param starting_pc: The starting pitch-class. If None, a random starting pitch-class is used.
\:return: A ten-trichord row
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.get_intervals
documentation:
Gets the interval sequence of a pcseg.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: The interval sequence
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.get_row_class
documentation:
Gets all of the rows in a row-class.
\:param row: A row
\:return: The row-class, as a list of rows
Compatible with PitchClasses mod 12 and 24

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.get_row_dsym
documentation:
Gets the degree of symmetry of a row.
\:param row: A row
\:return: The degree of symmetry of the row
Compatible with PitchClasses mod 12 and 24

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.imb_n
documentation:
Gets the IMB_n of a pcseg. The IMB_n is the segment of imbricated set-classes of cardinality n.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:param n: The cardinality of imbrication
\:return: The IMB_n
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.invert
documentation:
Inverts a pcseg.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: The inverted pcseg
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.is_all_interval_row
documentation:
Determines if a pcseg is an all-interval row.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: Whether or not the pcseg is an all-interval row
Compatible with PitchClasses mod 12 and 24

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.is_all_trichord_row
documentation:
Determines if a pcseg is an all-trichord row.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: Whether or not the pcseg is an all-trichord row
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.is_all_trichord_babbitt_row
documentation:
Determines if a pcseg is an "all-trichord" row (after Babbitt). This kind of row
contains ten unique imbricated trichords, none of which are set-classes [036] or [048].
Rotation is not considered.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: Whether or not the pcseg is an "all-trichord" row (after Babbitt)
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.is_link_chord
documentation:
Determines if a row is a Link chord (an all-interval row containing the all-trichord hexachord as a subset).
\:param pcseg: A pcset
\:return: True or False
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.is_row
documentation:
Determines if a pcseg is a row.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: Whether or not the pcseg is a row
Compatible with PitchClasses mod 12 and 24

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.is_row_generator
documentation:
Determines if a row generator is valid.
\:param rgen: A row generator
\:return: True if the row generator is valid; false otherwise
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.is_ten_trichord_row
documentation:
Determines if a pcseg is a ten-trichord row. This kind of row
contains ten unique imbricated trichords. Rotation is not considered.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: Whether or not the pcseg is an "all-trichord" row (after Babbitt)
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.make_pcseg12
documentation:
Makes a pcseg.
\:param args: Pcs
\:return: A pcseg
Compatible only with chromatic pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.make_pcseg24
documentation:
Makes a pcseg.
\:param args: Pcs
\:return: A pcseg
Compatible only with microtonal pcsegs

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.multiply
documentation:
Multiplies a pcseg.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:param n: The multiplier
\:return: The multiplied pcseg
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.multiply_order
documentation:
Multiplies a pcseg's order.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:param n: The multiplier
\:return: The order-multiplied pcseg
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.ormap
documentation:
Performs the ORMAP mapping on a row, given a provided ORMAP.
\:param row: A row
\:param ormap:
\:return: The ORMAP mapping for the row
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.prot
documentation:
Generates the protocol pairs for a pcseg.
\:param pcseg: A pcseg
\:return: A set of protocol pairs
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.retrograde
documentation:
Retrogrades a pcseg.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:return: The retrograded pcseg
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.rotate
documentation:
Rotates a pcseg.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:param n: The index of rotation
\:return: The rotated pcseg
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.transform
documentation:
Transforms a pcseg with a provided transformation string.
\- Tn: transpose
\- I: invert
\- Mn: multiply
\- R: retrograde
\- rn: rotate
\:param pcseg: The pcseg to transform
\:param string: The transformation string
\:return: The transformed pcseg

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.transform_hstack
documentation:
Generates a really long pcseg by horizontally stacking transformed versions of it.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg to stack
\:param transformation_list: A list of transformations to stack
\:return: The stacked and transformed pcseg

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.transpose
documentation:
Transposes a pcseg.
\:param pcseg: The pcseg
\:param n: The index of transposition
\:return: The transposed pcseg
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

functions.name: pctheory.pcseg.transpositional_combination
documentation:
Transpositionally combines (TC) two pcsegs. This is Boulez's "multiplication."
\:param pcseg1: A pcseg
\:param pcseg2: A pcseg
\:return: The TC pcset
Compatible with all PitchClass modulos

classes.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.__init__
documentation:
Creates an invariance matrix.
\:param a: Pcseg A
\:param c: Pcseg B
\:param mx_type: The matrix type (T, I, M, or MI)

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.__getitem__
documentation:
Gets the pc at the specified row and column.
\:param i: The row
\:param j: The column
\:return: The pc

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.__repr__
documentation:
Gets a representation of the InvarianceMatrix object.
:returns: A string representation of the InvarianceMatrix object

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.__str__
documentation:
Converts the InvarianceMatrix object to string.
:returns: A string conversion of the InvarianceMatrix object

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.mx
documentation:
Gets the invariance matrix.
\:return: The invariance matrix

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.pcseg_a
documentation:
Gets the pcseg A.
\:return: The pcseg A

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.pcseg_b
documentation:
Gets the pcseg B.
\:return: The pcseg B

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.pcseg_c
documentation:
Gets the pcseg C.
\:return: The pcseg C

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.get_column
documentation:
Gets a column of the matrix.
\:param j: The column index
\:return: The column

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.get_row
documentation:
Gets a row of the matrix.
\:param i: The row index
\:return: The row

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix._load_matrix
documentation:
Loads the matrix.
\:param a: Pcseg A
\:param c: Pcseg C
\:return: None

methods.name: pctheory.pcseg.InvarianceMatrix.print
documentation:
Prints the invariance matrix.
\:param include: The pcs to include (if None, all pcs will be printed)
\:returns: None

Represents an invariance matrix. Compatible with all PitchClass modulos for T and I
matrices, and compatible with mod 12 and mod 24 for various M matrices
(M5, M7 for mod 12; M5, M7, M11, M13, M17, M19 for mod 24)
